task_completion.description: Navigate to the specified project directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal prompt or file explorer showing the current directory is '/home/ga/zombie-debug'.

task_completion.description: Open the docker-compose.yml file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) or terminal output showing the contents of 'docker-compose.yml'.

task_completion.description: Modify docker-compose.yml to handle zombie processes (e.g., adding 'init: true').
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'docker-compose.yml' file is shown with 'init: true' added under the 'job-worker' service definition, or an equivalent init process wrapper (like tini) added to the command/entrypoint.

task_completion.description: Restart or recreate the Docker container to apply the compose file changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing execution of commands like 'docker-compose up -d', 'docker-compose up --force-recreate', or 'docker-compose restart' in the project directory.

task_completion.description: Ensure the job-worker service remains running after the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker ps' or 'docker-compose ps' with the 'job-worker' container in an 'Up' state.

task_completion.description: Verify that defunct/zombie processes are no longer accumulating.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a process list command (e.g., 'ps aux | grep defunct', 'top', or 'docker exec job-worker ps') with 2 or fewer '<defunct>' processes present.

integrity.description: Ensure the application code (worker.py) was not modified.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'worker.py' is never shown being edited in a text editor, and no terminal commands (like sed or echo) are used to alter its contents.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually interacted with the Docker daemon.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows genuine output from Docker commands (like container IDs, pull/recreate logs) rather than simulated or echoed text.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not just manually kill the zombies as a temporary workaround.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use 'kill -9' or similar commands on the defunct processes or their parent process in a loop to artificially lower the zombie count.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is '/home/ga/zombie-debug'. The Docker Compose service and container are both named 'job-worker'. The task strictly forbids modifying 'worker.py'. The correct solution involves modifying 'docker-compose.yml' (typically by adding 'init: true' to the job-worker service). The success condition requires the zombie process count (<defunct>) to be 2 or fewer.
